observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC - Municipal Accountant and the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Determine the cause of the material discrepancies in the Cash in Bank – LCCA accounts and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in their respective books; and
b. Conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made and/or errors detected by either of the offices will be adjusted / taken up in their respective books accordingly to ensure that the Cash in Bank – LCCA balance is correctly reported in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: The OIC – Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer explained that due to limited manpower and time constraints, they were not able to complete the reconciliation process. Nevertheless, they have mutually agreed to exert additional effort in performing regular reconciliations moving forward to ensure that the necessary adjustments are taken up in their respective books accordingly.
observations.reason: The source of the P3,849,106.66 has been identified and the same was recorded in the books of account under JEV Nos. 2024-07-002307 and 002308 dated July 1, 2024. Subsequently, record the transaction in the books of account and submit a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Prepare the required monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) to identify and reconcile the discrepancy between the Cash in Bank - LCCA per Subsidiary Ledger and bank balances aggregating to absolute amount of P28,633,623.14 pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011; and
b. Ensure that all identified valid reconciling items are properly recorded and accounted for in the books in compliance with Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: In the Management Reply dated May 30, 2025, the OIC - Municipal Accountant identified various reconciling items to be reflected in the books of accounts. However, she explained that there are still items which require further verification due to insufficient available information.
observations.reason: There are still items which require further verification due to insufficient available information.

observations.recommendation:

a. Determine the cause of the material discrepancies in the Cash in Bank – LCCA accounts and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in their respective books; and

Conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made and/or errors detected by either of the offices will be adjusted / taken up in their respective books accordingly to ensure that the Cash in Bank – LCCA balance is correctly reported in the Statement of Financial Position.

Nevertheless, they have mutually agreed to exert additional effort in performing regular reconciliations moving forward to ensure that the necessary adjustments are taken up in their respective books accordingly.
observations.reason: Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 2, Page Nos. 33 to 36, Part II of this Report. For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Officials submitted the written explanation regarding the granting and liquidation of cash advances. The Municipal Treasury Office, in coordination with the Municipal Accounting Office and under the directive of the Municipal Mayor, have taken actions to address the audit observations including issuance of a memorandum to all concerned offices reminding them of the liquidation timeline required by COA Circular No. 97-002 and the enforcement of strict controls to ensure no new cash advances are granted to those with unliquidated balances. The fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer has been increased to P22,000,000.00.
observations.reason: Only in February 2025, the Disbursing Officer was able to increase her maximum cash accountability. Some deficiencies on the granting and liquidation of cash advances remained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned contractors to refund the excess contract cost over the COA cost estimates, without prejudice to the issuance of the necessary Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer submitted the detailed results of re-evaluation by the COA Technical Audit Division of the Municipality. The Municipality was able to charge various PPAs to subsidize the cost and document adjustments.
The Municipalities have submitted additional supporting documents showing that all costs are advantageous to the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Audit Team is waiting for the final evaluation by the COA Technical Audit Division. A portion of the excess costs are still subject to review.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the OIC - Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will be the basis in recording or setting up the RPT/SET Receivable and its corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of the year;
b. OIC - Municipal Accountant to review its records to identify the noted discrepancy between the balances of RPT Receivables, SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Income and make the necessary adjustment, if warranted, to correct the balances of the accounts; and
c. OIC - Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile, analyze and investigate the cause/s of the discrepancy amounting to P52,009,193.66 between the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and the Certified List of Delinquent Taxes as of December 31, 2022 and make the necessary adjustments in their records, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The OIC – Municipal Accountant explained that NGAS 2.0.0 requires the receivables be recorded per barangay, and that the Acting Municipal Treasurer’s Office have provided them with the list needed to set-up the RPT and SET receivables. She also replied that the variance amounting to P7,700,502.20, between the RPT and SET receivables, was due to the different rates used for some RPU units. Further, the variance between the RPT Receivables and Deferred RPT Income was due to the unreconciled beginning balance from 2007 and advance payment of RPT amounting to P208,821.81 and P26,742.56, respectively. The municipal offices are coordinating to update and reconcile records.
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Accountant explained that they are still in the process of updating the certified list of taxpayers and checking the accounting records. There is inadequate manpower and substantial workload which delayed the full reconciliation. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 4, Page Nos. 39 to 41, Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer coordinated with taxpayers and issued receipts/AF No. 56 for identified collections. The Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant are evaluating the computerized RPT system and working to reclassify and correct entries. Some JEVs were prepared to record necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: The detailed list of taxpayers was not available. There is still ongoing coordination between the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile and adjust the accounts. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 4, Page Nos. 39 to 41, Part II of this Report. For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV to reclassify the receipts of Share from National Wealth, Share from PCSO and Financial Assistance from private sector to their proper accounts.
observations.managementAction: The OIC–Municipal Accountant submitted JEV No. 2024-05-0001065 dated May 3, 2024 to reclassify the recorded Share from National Wealth Tax.
observations.reason: Share from PCSO are still recorded under the Due to NGAs account. Adjustments are ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officers and
employees to:
a. Complete the one-time cleansing at the earliest possible time, without
compromising the accuracy and quality of the process, and in strict compliance
with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. Conduct annual physical count of all PPEs to ascertain its physical existence
and conditions pursuant to Section 124 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I and
prepare and submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31 of each year to the
COA;
c. Prepare the PPELCs and PCs pursuant to Sections 114 and 119 of MNGAS for
LGUs, Volume I, and ensure the reconciliation of the said property records; and
d. Apply a zero residual value for Road Networks pursuant to COA Circular No.
2015-008 and five percent residual value for all other depreciable assets
pursuant to PAG 6 of IPSAS 17, in the computation of depreciation.

We reiterated our audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC
- Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Determine the cause of the material discrepancies in the Cash in Bank – LCCA
accounts and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in their respective
books; and
b. Conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made
and/or errors detected by either of the offices will be adjusted / taken up in their
respective books accordingly to ensure that the Cash in Bank – LCCA balance
is correctly reported in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant,
in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
a. Prepare the required monthly BRS to identify and reconcile the discrepancy
between the Cash in Bank - LCCA per SL and bank balances aggregating to
absolute amount of P28,633,623.14 pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No.
96-011; and
b. Ensure that all identified valid reconciling items are properly recorded and
accounted for in the books in compliance with Section 3.3 of COA Circular No.
96-011.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the OIC - Municipal Accountant of a duly
certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible
for the year which will be the basis in recording or setting up the RPT/SET
Receivable and its corresponding Deferred RPT/SET Income at the beginning of
the year;
b. OIC - Municipal Accountant to review its records to identify the noted
discrepancy between the balances of RPT Receivables, SET Receivables and
Deferred RPT/SET Income and make the necessary adjustment, if warranted, to
correct the balances of the accounts; and
c. OIC - Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer to reconcile,
analyze and investigate the cause/s of the discrepancy amounting to
P23,977,841.08 between the balances of the RPT and SET Receivables and the
Certified List of Delinquent Taxes as of December 31, 2024 and make the
necessary adjustments in their records, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC - Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Use zero residual value in the computation of the amortization of Intangible
Assets and draw the adjusting journal entry to properly reflect the correct
amounts of the affected accounts, pursuant to Paragraph 99 of IPSAS 31 and
Section 112 of PD No. 1445; and
b. Update the current system being used of the details of the Intangible Assets to
ensure proper monitoring and accounting for the asset.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC - Municipal Accountant
to ensure that reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds,
are reconciled, otherwise, make necessary and timely adjustments in the books to
reflect the correct account balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officers and employees to:
a. Inventory Committee (IC) to conduct annual physical count of all PPEs to ascertain its physical existence and conditions pursuant to Section 124, Volume I, of MNGAS for LGUs and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) on or before January 31 of each year to the COA;
b. IC, in coordination with the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Office (GSO), to expedite the implementation of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE account balances in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
c. OIC-Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expense of the PPE, except for road networks, using the five percent residual value and make the necessary correcting entries in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee (IC) is now taking appropriate actions and measures to ensure that complete one-time cleansing procedures will be undertaken. Additionally, the IC has formally communicated with the General Services Office and the Accounting Office, requesting their coordination in the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the property and accounting records, as well as, the preparation and maintenance of PPELC and PCs.
Moreover, the OIC – Municipal Acocuntant have submiited a request to eTicketing System for the development and implementation of the necessary script to ensure accurate computation of all depreciable PPEs.
observations.reason: For preparation of Recommendation Tracking Sheet (RTS) for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the complete set of financial statements together with its supporting schedules as required by Paragraph 21 of IPSAS 1, Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004, Sections 2.1 and 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 and Section 76, Chapter IV, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The OIC – Municipal Accountant submitted the complete set of CY 2024 Financial Statements within the reglementary period.

The MPDC, in coordination with other concerned department heads, are taking extra measures to ensure that PPAs included in the AIP are feasible. The Municipal Engineer submitted his justification to the Audit Team. Moreover, various PPAs from National Wealth Taxes were charged as part of the Municipality.
observations.reason: A portion of the outstanding balance of the 80 percent from National Wealth was not appropriated to specific PPAs. The required reports and supporting documents were not properly maintained or submitted.

observations.managementAction:
In a letter dated May 26, 2025, the Acting Municipal Treasurer committed that, in coordination with the Municipal Accounting Office and under the directive of the Municipal Mayor, all concerned officers and employees will be instructed to ensure the prompt and full liquidation of all cash advances before year-end, as well as to comply with all other audit recommendations.
In compliance with TC No. 002-2019, the fidelity bond of the disbursing officer has also been increased to P22,000,000.00 as of this date, and a copy of the updated fidelity bond certificate has been provided.

The Municipal Administrator submitted a response to the COA. The concerned consultants provided their qualifications and outputs. The Municipality has compiled the CVs and outputs as required and submitted them to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal Officials to explain the different daily rates of hired JO personnel and review the Municipality’s existing policies relative to the hiring and compensation of JO personnel to adhere with the minimum wage regulations and standards on labor practices.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Officials submitted the written explanation regarding the different rates of JO personnel. The Municipal Administrator clarified that rates were based on job complexity and funding sources and committed to align future hiring with prevailing wage orders.

The concerned officials of the Municipality submitted the written explanation. The Local Development Council are taking steps to ensure proper vetting of PPAs. The Municipal Engineer and MPDC are coordinating to ensure PPAs included in the AIP are feasible and procurement ready.
observations.reason: Some PPAs were reprogrammed. Reiterated with modifications in Audit Observation No. 8, Page Nos. 46 to 50, Part II of this Report. For preparation of RTS for recommendations considered closed for having been reiterated with modifications.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. Local Finance Committee to include all estimated revenues from the Municipality in the computation of the appropriations for LDRRMF pursuant to Section 21 of RA No. 10121; and
b. LDRRM Officer and other concerned officials to strictly adhere with all the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF, particularly in the preparation and submission of LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization, maintenance of Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO), full disclosure of LDRRMF in the NTFS, and ensure that all figures and information provided are accurate and reliable.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee includes all the estimated revenue of the Municipality in the computation of the appropriations for LDRRMF. The LDRRM officer and concerned officials have complied with the noted deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Budget Officer to allocate at least five percent of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD related PPAs as required under Section 36(a) of RA No. 9710.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Budget Officer allocated at least five percent of the total annual budget for GAD related PPAs and submitted the necessary documentation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Withhold taxes on payments to consultancies pursuant to Section 57 of NIRC as amended and Section 2 of BIR RR No. 11-2018 and apply the correct withholding tax rate based on the submitted documents of the payees; and
b. Ensure the remittance of the withheld taxes to BIR.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department is amenable with the audit finding and withholds taxes from the payments to consultants using the applicable rate of either five percent or ten percent. As of April 2024, the Municipality withholds and remits taxes from the payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required BRS in order to determine the P43,249,953.09 discrepancy of the Cash in Bank - LCCA per general ledger (GL) and bank balances and make the necessary adjustments in the books in compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Section 3.2 of the COA Circular No. 96-011; otherwise, an administrative sanction shall be imposed for repeated unjustified failure to comply with the previous years’ audit recommendations pursuant to Section 127 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant explained that she is now personally investigating the causes of the discrepancy between the Cash in Bank – LCCA and bank balances. The OIC-Municipal Accountant explained that she is now personally investigating the causes of the discrepancy between the Cash in Bank – LCCA and bank balances.
observations.reason: Due to the unidentified and numerous items investigating the discrepancy insufficient manpower and current workload of accounting personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Determine the material discrepancies in the Cash in Bank – LCCA accounts and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in their respective books; and b. Conduct regular reconciliation of their balances so that the adjustments made and/or errors detected by either of the offices will be adjusted / taken up in their respective books accordingly to ensure that the Cash in Bank – LCCA balance is correctly reported in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: Upon examination of the OIC-Municipal Treasurer of the cashbook records, she stated that several discrepancies were due to erroneous recordings, incorrect computations of month-end balances and unrecorded items. She will update and adjust the cashbooks once all erroneous and unrecorded transactions were finally identified. The OIC-Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to coordinate and conduct regular reconciliation of their respective books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to: a. furnish the Municipal Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; b. review the process and data stored in the Municipality’s accounting system and further explain the noted discrepancy between the balances of RPT Receivables and SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET and make the necessary adjustment, if warranted, to correct the balances of both accounts; c. coordinate and reconcile and investigate the cause/s of the deficiency amounting to P52,009,193.66 between the balances of the RPT and SET receivables and the Certified List of Delinquent Taxes as of December 31, 2022 and make the necessary adjustments in their records, if warranted. Municipal Accountant to: d. Make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of the Municipality to correct the entries made pertaining to advance payments for RPT; and e. Record the receipt of RPT from taxpayer with discount for advanced payment using prescribed entries.
observations.managementAction: The OIC - Municipal Treasurer furnished the OIC - Municipal Accountant with details of the amounts due and collectibles. The OIC-Municipal Accountant replied that they are still in the process of updating and checking the details of the RPT/SET Receivables. The OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer are coordinating to reconcile the variances. Joint efforts are being done by the OIC-Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the RPT/SET balances. However, some supporting documents could not be located.
observations.reason: Not Due to the unidentified and numerous items and lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. Coordinate with the taxpayers who paid the advance payments to facilitate the issuance of the corresponding AF No. 56; and with the Accounting Office for the transfer of the remaining balance of the advanced payment from the TF to GF and SEF accounts; b. Transfer the remaining balance of advance RPT collections from the bank account maintained in the TF to the corresponding bank accounts in GF and SEF; c. Issue RPT Receipt or AF No. 56 for all the collections/ payments received pertaining to RPT and deposit the same under the bank accounts maintained in the GF and SEF; and d. Evaluate the existing computerized RPT System and recommend the need for replacement or upgrade to improve the efficiency of recording collections. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant: e. Make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of the Municipality to correct the entries made pertaining to advance payments for RPT; and f. Record the receipt of RPT from taxpayer with discount for advanced payment using the following entry: Debit: Cash Local Treasury; Discount on Advance Payment of RPT; Credit: Deferred RPT/SET Income; Due to LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Treasurer is coordinating with taxpayers who availed the advance payment of RPT to issue AF No. 56 and process the transfer of the remaining balance. The OIC-Municipal Accountant is to implement the correct process and will record necessary adjusting entries. The Municipality is reviewing the RPT process and will implement changes starting CY 2024.
observations.reason: Not Implemented: No action was taken in CY 2023; implementation to start in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the receipts of Share from National Wealth, Share from PCSO and Financial Assistance with specific purpose from Due to NGAs to their proper accounts.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant did not take action earlier due to voluminous tasks upon assumption of office but later prepared adjusting entries.
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Accountant was overwhelmed with voluminous tasks when she assumed the Accounting Office and she overlooked the adjustments; later adjustments were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to locate the pertinent records and documents and review the previous years’ recorded Accounts Payable to ensure that only legitimate and valid claims are recorded in the books, otherwise, cause the reversion of those payables aged two years or more which are determined to be without valid claimants/creditors pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant is exerting effort to locate the pertinent records and documents of the previous years and submitted several JEVs recorded in CY 2024 with an aggregate amount of P1,891,684.63 adjusting some items included in the audit observation.
observations.reason: Insufficient manpower and voluminous workload; some items remain pending verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to review and examine all the remaining abnormal balances appearing in the subsidiary ledgers of Accounts Payable, Due to Officers and Employees, Due to NGAs, Due to LGUs and Other Payables accounts and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections, if warranted, for fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office submitted several JEVs recorded in CY 2024 adjusting some items. The OIC-Municipal Accountant assures the Audit Team that the remaining negative balances with absolute value of P255,105.91 will be adjusted upon verification of its source and correct entry to record the transactions.
observations.reason: Deficient manpower and heavy workload causing delay in reconciliation and adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to disclose the required information in the Notes to Financial Statements to assist the users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with the additional information value.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant assured that they will comply with the audit recommendation and submitted additional disclosures subsequently.
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Accountant just assumed the Accounting Office recently and is still preparing the required disclosures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to plan and
develop PPAs and incorporate it in the AWP, AIP and APP of the Municipality
pursuant to Section 294 of RA No. 7160, Sections 3 and 5 Rule 28 B of the IRR
of RA No. 9136, Section 21 (B), Rule 7 of the IRR of RA No. 9513 and DOE DC No.
DC2018-08-2001;
b. Request the Sanggunian to appropriate the proceeds from National Wealth
Taxes of the Municipality to finance eligible PPAs according to Section 294 of
RA No. 7160;
c. Instruct the MPDC, Municipal Engineer and other concerned Municipal Officials
to effectively and efficiently utilize the fund by implementing the PPAs included
in the AWP/AIP/APP within the specific target dates; and
d. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and other concerned Municipal Officials
to prepare and submit the required reports and supporting documents such as,
but not limited to, summary of transactions funded by the share from national
wealth, utilization report, implementation status of the projects and the like.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. Send demand letters to the Accountable Officers with unliquidated cash
advances;
b. Withhold the salary of those AOs who still failed to liquidate their cash
advances without valid reason despite demands made;
c. Strictly monitor the liquidation of the cash advances granted to the AOs and
ensure that all CA are fully liquidated at the end of each year in accordance with
COA Circular No. 97-002; and
d. Immediately submit the two (2) unsubmitted DVs with respect to Advances to
Officers and Employees related to Trainings and Seminars.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officers and
employees to:
a. Strictly implement Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and all the pertinent provisions of
COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant and liquidation of cash advances;
b. Review and improve the process of the preparation and approval of payrolls to
reduce the number of payrolls and granting of multiple cash advances to the
same DO;
c. Stop the practice of designating or assigning the disbursement or pay-out of
birthday cash gift incentive for Senior Citizens to Job Order personnel of the
Municipality;
d. Comply with the documentary requirements required by COA Circular No. 2023-
004, specifically, the issuance of Certification from the Chief Accountant that
previous cash advances have been fully liquidated and accounted for in the
books; and
e. Increase the fidelity bond of the concerned Disbursing Officer and ensure that
she is properly bonded by in accordance with Treasury Circular No. 002-2019,
otherwise, appoint or designate another DO for the grant of additional cash
advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned contractors to
refund the excess in the contract cost over the COA cost estimates, without prejudice to
the issuance of the necessary Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor and other concerned Municipal Officials to:
a. Submit written explanation or justification on the necessity of hiring the
consultants in such a way that their employment did not create duplication of work
already performed by existing agency work force; otherwise, consultancy fees paid
to these consultants will be disallowed in audit;
b. Provide or submit the qualifications or special skills of the consultants which
made them qualified as “experts in an acknowledged authority in their field of
specialization”;
c. Provide the basis in determining the reasonableness of the monthly
compensation of the consultants; and
d. Provide the Municipal Accountant of the copy of curriculum vitae of the
consultants, Accomplishment Reports or outputs as required under the contract
and letter request for payment of each consultant as supporting documents to
the disbursement vouchers pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 as amended
by COA Circular No. 2023-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal Officials to
explain the different daily rates of hired JO personnel and review the Municipality’s
existing policies relative to the hiring and compensation of JO personnel to adhere with
the minimum wage regulations and standards on labor practices issued by Civil
Service Commission and other regulatory government agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to: a. Submit all the working papers relative to the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts; b. Completely adopt the property identification system for PPE and tag all PPE items counted with new property identification stickers; c. Immediately submit RPCPPE as of December 31, 2022 to the Audit Team and other concerned offices; d. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to fully implement and undertake the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts; and Municipal Accountant to: e. Comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004 and record the necessary adjusting entries; f. Exert more effort to verify the existence and validity of the recorded amounts in the PPE accounts of the Municipality without valid supporting documents; g. Fast track the reconciliation of the PPE, analyze and evaluate any discrepancies and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of PPE accounts; h. Follow all other procedures deemed necessary such as the disposition procedure for identified “non-existing/missing” PPE and its derecognition from the books and submit all prescribed reports pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; and i. Religiously update and maintain the PPELCs and maintain detailed lapsing schedule for all PPE items.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee submitted RPCPPE as of December 31, 2022. The IC admitted that annual physical inventory for CY 2023 was not conducted. The Municipality discontinued the one-time cleansing in CY 2022 due to unavailability and work constraints and will redo the implementation. Various JEVs dated January 2, 2023 were drawn to adjust semi-expendable properties.
observations.reason: Not completed one-time cleansing of PPE accounts; lack of manpower and substantial workload delayed full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor to coordinate with the Provincial Assessor to conduct the reevaluation and reassessment of the appraised values of the properties under the Municipality's jurisdiction based on the prevailing market rates and mapping and thereafter make necessary revisions every three years pursuant to Sections 201 and 219 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor submitted updates that they are coordinating with the Provincial Assessor and have begun tax mapping in various barangays since September 2022; they appropriated P1.5M for tax mapping in CY 2023 and coordinated with Notaries Public to gather deeds and other documents for updating market values.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, Human Resource and Management Officer (HRMO) and other concerned Municipal Officials to: a. Adopt the use of ATM or Cash Card in the payment of wages of JO personnel to install tighter internal control of its payroll funds, reduce the workload and working hours spent by the DO in the preparation of the payroll and liquidation reports and eliminate withdrawal of large sum of money for payroll purposes; and b. Request the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) Manager for the enrollment of the 67 JO personnel to ATM payroll or Cash Card account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality adopted the use of ATMs and cash cards for payment of JO wages. The JO personnel were enrolled for ATM payroll/cash cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned Municipal Officials to strictly comply with all the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004, in particular: a. Prepare and submit within the prescribed period a complete list of all on-going and to be implemented government PPAs as mandated by Item 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004; b. For infrastructure and non-infrastructure PPAs, notify the public of the Municipality’s PPA through posting of tarpaulins/signages on project sites; and c. Notify the Audit Team within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning Coordinator submitted the list of all on-going and completed government PPAs as of December 31, 2022 on February 21, 2023. Pictures showing tarpaulins/signages were installed at project sites and were submitted. No new infrastructure projects funded by the Municipality were noted during CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to require contractors, upon payment of 15 percent mobilization fee, to submit irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand as supporting documents for release of advance payments to contractors.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office included the required irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank or bank guarantee or surety bond as supporting documents for the release of advance payments to contractors and observed proper recoupment of advance payments pursuant to Section 4.3 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to recoup the advance payments made to contractors pursuant to Section 4.3 of Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Proper recoupment of advance payments was observed. The Accounting Office implemented corrective measures and recouped advance payments as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the ten percent (10%) retention money from each progress billing and that the total retention money be released only upon final acceptance of the project or upon the release request of the contractor to substitute the same with an irrevocable standby letter of credit or surety bond callable on demand as provided in Section 6.2 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: It was observed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant only released the retention money upon final acceptance of the project. The Accounting Office assured that sanctions will be imposed for contractors who failed to comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to correctly compute and withhold the five percent final withholding tax and two percent expanded withholding tax on all its payment for infrastructure projects based on the progress billing or gross payment net of VAT as required by Item C of Section 114 of the NIRC and Item J of Section 2.57.2 of BIR RR No. 11-2018.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is now withholding the applicable rates prescribed by the BIR in every payment pertaining to infrastructure projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to furnish the original copy of reports to the DA-RFO IV-A and to strictly comply with the provisions under Sections 6.5 and 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality furnished the original copies of liquidation reports and disbursements to DA RFO IV-A; the unspent amount of P1,358,628.80 from the total fund of P4,835,800.00 for different projects was already returned to DA RFO IV-A with Check No. 1256001 dated June 27, 2022. Copies of liquidation and disbursement reports were furnished to the concerned government office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned personnel to: a. Conduct appropriate detailed engineering before the commencement of the procurement process of any infrastructure projects to determine all issues and factors that might affect the viability of the projects in accordance with Annex “A” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; b. Faithfully monitor and evaluate the implementation of the projects so that any cause of suspension or problems will be immediately addressed and to prevent contract termination; c. Fast track the implementation of its ongoing and suspended projects and to explain the cause of its delays and submit the approved suspension orders, if any; and d. In all cases of delays of the contractor which are found to be unjustified, impose liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted explanations and committed to conduct detailed engineering for every project. Projects were monitored and additional justification for delays were submitted. Some projects were completed; others remained suspended due to issues such as right-of-way and coordination with affected residents. The Accounting Office assured it will impose liquidated damages to contractors found to be at fault.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned officials to immediately submit the lacking documents and explain the deficiencies noted for transactions with aggregate amount of P41,381,215.66 to the Audit Team within 15 days for further review and evaluation; and henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted various project documents showing computations and explanations for the noted deficiencies. The documents were forwarded to COA Technical Audit Section for reevaluation and reconsideration. If found not valid, refund should be made by the concerned contractors/personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the concerned Municipal Officials, to: a. Submit further written explanations and justifications on the low utilization or non-implementation of the priority PPAs funded under the local development fund; b. Effectively and judiciously plan the priority development programs, projects and activities to be included in the AIP by considering their feasibility and necessity; c. Strictly observe and follow the timeline or specific date of the project first reviewed and implementation as indicated in the APP; d. Maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund to optimally achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer submitted explanations and the Municipality realigned various PPAs during CY 2023. The Management committed to enhance planning and to ensure PPAs included in the AIP are feasible and necessary. New PPAs were reprogrammed and are being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRMC to immediately decide on the use of the unexpended balance which shall be decided upon by the LDRRMC and incorporate the same in the LDRRMFIP pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMC is yet to select PPAs to be funded out of the unexpended balances; actions are ongoing. The Municipality is coordinating and plans to include such PPAs in the LDRRMIP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. Local Finance Committee to include all estimated revenues from regular sources of the Municipality in the computation of the appropriations for LDRRMF pursuant to Section 21 of RA No. 10121; and b. LDRRM Officer and other concerned officials to strictly adhere with all the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF, particularly in the preparation and submission of LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization, maintenance of Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO), full disclosure of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements, and ensure that all figures and information provided are accurate and reliable.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee committed to review proper computation of resource allocation to prevent insufficient funding of the minimum five percent appropriation to LDRRMF and to implement stronger internal controls. LDRRM Officer and other concerned officials agreed with the observations and committed to comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting Municipal Budget Officer to allocate at least five percent of the total annual budget for the implementation of GAD related PPAs as required under Section 36 (a) of RA No. 9710 or “The Magna Carta of Women”.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Budget Officer assured that all regular sources of revenues will be considered in the computation of the required five percent allocation for GAD Fund and the Local Finance Committee included at least 5% of the Municipality’s total budget for GAD programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to withhold taxes on payments to Consultancy Services pursuant to Section 57 of NIRC as amended and Section 2 of BIR No. 11-2018 and apply the correct withholding tax rate based on the submitted documents of the payees.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Department is amenable with the audit finding and recommendation. As of April 2024, the Municipality withholds taxes from the payments to consultants using the applicable rate of either five percent (5%) or ten percent (10%).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to:
• prepare Schedule of Unreleased Checks monthly and forward the same to the Municipal Accountant; and
• send notice to the concerned payees regarding the existence of the subject checks.
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher at year-end to record the restoration of unreleased checks and recognize the corresponding liability account to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the schedule of unreleased checks. No action was taken by the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer overlooked the preparation of the schedule of unreleased checks and sending of notices. The Municipal Accountant overlooked preparing the journal entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to prioritize the inclusion of PPAs which are much needed by the Municipality and implementable during the year in the preparation of LDRRMFIP to strengthen its capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks; and strictly implement the PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP to fully optimize the utilization of the Mitigation Fund.
observations.managementAction: Due to poor planning and COVID-19 pandemic restrictions, most of the PPAs were not implemented.
observations.reason: Most PPAs were not implemented due to poor planning and restrictions brought by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to provide the documents and schedules requested by
the Municipal Accountant in order to facilitate the preparation of the BRS; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary BRS in order to determine
the causes of the discrepancy of (P24,343,352.86) between the SL and bank
balances and thereafter make the necessary adjustments in the books.
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
c.1 determine the causes of the discrepancy of P131,844,734.62 between
their records and make the necessary adjustments accordingly; and
c.2 conduct periodic reconciliation of their records so that errors can be
promptly detected and corrected, thus ensuring the fair presentation of
the Cash in Bank accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical count of all PPEs and
submit the required RPCPPE in the prescribed template to the Office of the
Auditor not later than January 31 of the ensuing year;
b. General Services Officer to ensure that all of the semi-expendable properties
which are still existing are covered by ICS for monitoring and control
purposes;
c. Municipal Accountant to:
c.1 draw Journal Entry Voucher to drop the semi-expendable properties
from the books by debiting the Government Equity and Accumulated
Depreciation of the respective PPE, and crediting the respective PPE
accounts in the General Fund books in the amounts of P246,998.98,
P949,059.52 and P1,196,058.50, respectively; and in the Special
Education Fund books in the amounts of P131.05, P8,558.95 and
P8,690.00, respectively;
c.2 exert efforts to secure the necessary documents to support the
undepreciated PPEs by tracing back all available records and prepare
thereafter lapsing schedule of PPE to compute the unrecognize
depreciation; and
c.3 draw Journal Entry Voucher to record the unrecognized depreciation by
debiting Government Equity and crediting the corresponding
Accumulated Depreciation by the amount computed in the lapsing
schedule of PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to:
a.1 prepare Schedule of Unreleased Checks monthly and forward the same
to the Municipal Accountant; and
a.2 send notice to the concerned payees regarding the existence of the
subject checks.
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) at year-end
to record the restoration of unreleased checks and recognize the
corresponding liability account to reflect the correct balances of the affected
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. demand the immediate liquidation of the outstanding CAs to Officers and
Employees totaling P244,189.00;
b. refrain from allowing liquidation of CAs without immediately refunding the
excess/unutilized balance thereof; and
c. ensure that all CAs are liquidated at the end of each year pursuant to Item 5.8
of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and submit to the Audit Team a complete set of year-end financial
reports and documents within the prescribed period in accordance with the
aforementioned guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to:
a. immediately submit the 73 DVs and its supporting documents included in the
list of unsubmitted DVs (Appendix G) to avoid issuance of Notice of
Suspensions and/or Notice of Disallowances; and
b. strictly observe the prescribed period in the submission of paid DVs and its
supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor in compliance with
Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
and General Services Officer to immediately comply with the full implementation
of the perpetual inventory method in the recording and reporting of all procured
goods, supplies and materials for distribution and consumption pursuant to the
pertinent provisions of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I, to ensure proper internal
controls in the management of the inventories of the Municipality, as well as, the
accurate recognition of inventory and expense accounts in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to make necessary journal entry to correct the
misclassification of accounts and ensure that the succeeding receipt of
funds related to the disaster risk reduction and management program shall
be recorded in the Trust Liability-DRRM account pursuant to COA Circular
No. 2012-002; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to remit the unutilized funds of P1,992,355.98 back to the
BTr pursuant to Item of 3.7 of LBC No. 130.

a. Municipal Accountant to:
• follow-up with the LBP - San Pablo City Branch the credentials needed for the weAccess internet facility and secure the data required for the preparation of monthly BRS;
• prepare the lacking BRS based on the weAccess internet facility and secure the data required for the preparation of monthly BRS; and
• determine the causes of the discrepancy of P46,169,933.78 between the GL and bank balance and make the necessary adjustments in the books.
b. Municipal Treasurer to prepare an independent reconciliation separate from that of the Accounting to determine the causes of the discrepancy of P34,070,127.89 between the CB and bank balance and make the necessary adjustments in her records; and
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
• determine the causes of the discrepancy of P12,099,805.89 between their records and make the necessary adjustments therein; and
• conduct periodic reconciliation of their records so that errors can be promptly detected and corrected, thus ensuring the fair presentation of the Cash in Bank accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office has tried accessing the weAccess facility but was denied due to incorrect credentials. The Municipal Accounting Office is still waiting for the new login credentials.
observations.reason: No action was taken by the Management. No additional records were gathered, hence the preparation of the BRS was not undertaken. Determination of the causes of discrepancy is still in process pending the preparation of the BRS.

observations.managementAction: The Acting General Services Officer explained that he is only new to the position and that the members of the Inventory Committee has other regular duties to perform. The Municipal Accountant committed to make the recommended adjusting entries in the books.
observations.reason: Some of the semi-expendable properties were covered with the necessary ICS. Securing of documents is still ongoing. Lapsing schedule of PPE was still not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the remaining balance of fund transfers from the City Government of Pasig totaling P4,525,133.69 from Due to NGAs to Due to LGUs account to present the account balance in the financial statements properly.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher No. 2021-07-000355 dated July 10, 2021 to record reclassification.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to:
a. cause the immediate submission of the 55 unsubmitted DVs to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension, which may mature into disallowance; and
b. observe timeliness in the submission of paid DVs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the end of the month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant were able to submit the 55 DVs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

➢ Debit Due to NGAs account and credit Government Equity account both by P1,237,447.37;
➢ Debit Government Equity account and credit Due to NGAs account both by P47,900.00.
• cause the reversion of the unutilized balance of the BGCM amounting to P7,426.11 to the National Treasury.
b. Municipal Budget Officer to always include information on the Obligation Request as to the fund used for a particular expenditure for its proper recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the recommended adjusting entries. The Municipal Budget Officer now includes information as to the fund used.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. demand the immediate liquidation of the outstanding CAs to Officers and Employees totaling P244,189.00;
b. refrain from allowing liquidation of CAs without immediately refunding the excess/unutilized balance thereof; and

observations.managementAction: Most of the CAs granted were liquidated by the accountable officers within the prescribed period. The Municipal Accountant will require accountable officers to submit supporting documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Liquidations of CAs with huge amounts take time to complete. Incomplete documents to support liquidations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. exert extra efforts in identifying the nature of the balance of the Deferred RPT account of P208,821.81 by tracing back available records of previous years and effect the necessary adjustments, if any;
b. draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the accumulated variance of P2.56 in the General Fund books by debiting Deferred RPT account and crediting Government Equity account both by the same amount; and
c. immediately adjust the incurred slight variance every year-end between the RPT Receivable and Deferred RPT caused by the deficiencies in the e-NGAS.
observations.managementAction: The noted slight variance was adjusted in the records of the Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant is securing relevant documents/records to support the noted balance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Requesting Unit to submit the required lacking documents complete or supplied with relevant information on the transactions listed in Appendix J; and ensure that all claims are paid based on duly processed DVs with complete supporting documents.
b. Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee to immediately post the noted 40 procurement projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement to the GPPB Online Portal.
observations.managementAction: The noted lacking documents were submitted to the Audit Team. The Municipal procurement projects were posted in the GPPB Online Portal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly require the accountable officers to liquidate their CAs supported with complete documentary requirements within the period prescribed under COA Circulars.
observations.managementAction: Most of the CAs granted were liquidated by the accountable officers within the prescribed period. Some outstanding documentary requirements remain.
observations.reason: Some CAs with large amounts take time to complete necessary supporting documents.

observations.managementAction: Most of the PPAs included in the Annual Investment Plan were already implemented by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Some of the projects remained unimplemented due to lack of technical expertise of concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to strictly:
a. implement the PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget to fully optimize the fund's utilization; and
b. adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 on the timely submission of the GPB and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Office of the Auditor within five working days from the receipt of the approved plan from the DILG and five working days from the end of January of the preceding year, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The required GAD Plan and GAD Budget and Accomplishment Report were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Most planned PPAs were not implemented due to COVID-19 restrictions; however, required reports were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and the Provincial Treasurer to determine the cause/s of discrepancies of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality as against their respective reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province and effect the necessary adjustments accordingly in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer are to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to determine the causes of the differences and effect necessary adjustments; however, reconciliation could not be fully conducted due to restrictions brought by the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: Reconciliation with the Provincial Government accounts could not be completed due to restrictions brought by the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management explain the EME paid in excess of the prescribed limit totaling P493,583.90, otherwise, the amount will be disallowed in the audit.

undertaken through Emergency Procurement to the GPPB Online Portal, in
compliance with the pertinent provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and
GPPB-TSO Advisory No. 05-2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
strictly require the accountable officers to liquidate their CAs supported with
complete documentary requirements within the period prescribed under COA
Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer to:
a. stop the practice of charging regular expenditures against the LDF, otherwise,
the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit;
b. appropriate and utilize the LDF for PPAs that contribute to the attainment of
socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes which
partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures; and
c. strictly implement the development projects included in the Annual
Development Plan to fully optimize the utilization of the LDF and achieve
desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to:
a. prioritize the inclusion of PPAs which are much needed by the Municipality and
implementable during the year in the preparation of LDRRMFIP to strengthen its
capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks; and
b. strictly implement the PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP to fully optimize the
utilization of the Mitigation Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
strictly implement the PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget to fully
optimize the fund's utilization.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to
strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 on the timely submission of
the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to the Office of the
Auditor within five working days from the receipt of the approved plan from the DILG
and five working days from the end of January of the preceding year,
respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• request from the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) - San Pablo City Branch an LBP weAccess account to retrieve copies of missing/unlocated BS needed for the preparation of monthly BRS;
• prepare the lacking BRS based on the available records and BS requested from the bank, if received, and regularly submit to the Office of the Auditor; and
• determine the causes of the discrepancy of P20,214,551.11 between the SL and bank balance based on the prepared BRS and make the necessary adjustments in the books.
b. Municipal Treasurer to:
• provide the Municipal Accountant with all available documents necessary for the preparation of monthly BRS; and
• determine the causes of the discrepancy between the CB and bank balance amounting to P16,046,721.11 and make the necessary adjustments in the cash book.
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
• determine the causes of the discrepancy of P4,167,830.36 between their records and make the necessary adjustments therein; and

observations.managementAction: The Municipality already applied for an LBP weAccess account to retrieve copies of missing/unlocated BS needed for the preparation of monthly BRS. The Municipal Accounting Office was able to prepare the BRS for January 2019 based on available records and BS requested from the bank, if received, and will regularly submit to the Office of the Auditor. The Municipal Treasurer provided available documents to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of monthly BRS.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still waiting for the LBP weAccess credentials to be provided by LBP – San Pablo Branch. Determination of the causes of the noted discrepancies between the SL, CB and BS are still in process pending preparation of lacking BRS and reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to drop the semi-expendable property from the books by debiting Government Equity and Accumulated Depreciation of respective PPE, and by crediting the respective PPE accounts in the General Fund books in the amounts of P246,998.98, P949,059.52 and P1,196,058.50, respectively; and in the Special Education Fund books in the amounts of P131.05, P8,558.95 and P8,690.00, respectively.
b. Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all of the semi-expendable property that are still existing are covered by Inventory Custodian Slip for monitoring and control purposes.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. secure documents to support the undepreciated PPEs by tracing back all available records and prepare thereafter lapsing schedule of PPE to compute the unrecognized depreciation; and
b. draw Journal Entry Voucher to record the unrecognized depreciation by debiting Government Equity and crediting the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation by the amount computed in the lapsing schedule of PPE.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to effecting the necessary adjustments in the books to drop the semi-expendable property included in the PPE accounts. The Municipal General Services Officer is also committed to maintaining ICS for all issued semi-expendable property. The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with audit recommendations on the provision of depreciation for the noted PPEs.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant admitted he overlooked the implementation of some recommendations and securing documents to support undepreciated PPEs is still ongoing; lapsing schedule of PPE was still not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
• immediately transfer the unexpected balances of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE totaling P8,346,437.28 for CY 2019 to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund books; and
• disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the details and amount of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) as of year-end under the Special Trust Fund.
b. Municipal Budget Officer to furnish the Municipal Accounting Office with a copy of the SAAOB as required under Item 4.2 of every year-end.
observations.managementAction: The unspent balance of QRF of P3,012,982.74 was already transferred to the Special Trust Fund of the Trust Fund. The Municipal Budget Officer is now furnishing the Municipal Accounting Office a copy of SAAOB as required. The Municipal Accountant was still unable to disclose the details of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements due to variance between the utilization report and the book balance.
observations.reason: Preparation of the detailed disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements is pending due to variance between the utilization report and the book balance; full disclosure not yet effected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the remaining balance of fund transfers from the City Government of Pasig totaling P5,266,549.75 from Due to NGAs to Due to LGUs account to properly present the account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to effecting the necessary adjustments in the books to reclassify the fund transfers from City Government of Pasig from Due to NGAs to Due to LGUs account.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant admitted he overlooked implementation of the recommendation and has yet to complete reclassification adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to fasten the preparation of Report of Checks Issued and transmit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the 338 DVs and its supporting documents to the Accounting Office for compilation and submission to the Office of the Auditor to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension; and
b. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to observe timeliness in the submission of paid DVs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the end of the month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted many of the DVs and their supporting documents; the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accounting Office coordinated to compile and forward to the Office of the Auditor. However, there is still a delay in submission of 55 DVs for CY 2020 ranging from 142 to 417 days as some DVs were missing/unlocated.
observations.reason: There is still a delay in the submission of 55 DVs for CY 2020, ranging from 142 to 417 days. The Municipal Accounting Office explained that those DVs were missing/unlocated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to properly charge payments to JO personnel under the Other General Services account instead of Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses; and
b. Municipal Budget Officer to allocate payments for the services of JO personnel under the Other General Services account in the annual budget instead of to the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account.
observations.managementAction: Payments for the services of JO personnel are now allocated in the annual budget under the Other General Services account. The Municipal Accountant was not able to convert the payments to JO personnel previously recorded under Other MOOE to Other General Services account during preparation of financial statements but corrective allocation has been effected in the annual budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. ensure that all CAs are liquidated at the end of each year pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; and
b. issue demand letter for the immediate liquidation of CAs of personnel who resigned/retired/transferred to another office/agency and prevent the granting of additional CA to any official, officer or employee without liquidating first his/her prior CA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that granting additional CA without liquidation first is now prohibited and committed to coordinate with concerned officers to liquidate outstanding CAs. The Municipal Accounting Office coordinated with the former District Supervisor to secure documents/clearance for outstanding CAs.
observations.reason: A total of P422,989.00 CAs remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2020 due to incomplete supporting documents and transfers/resignations of accountable officers; clearance from money and property accountabilities for outgoing personnel not always secured prior to transfer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. exert extra efforts in identifying the nature of the balance of the Deferred RPT account of P208,821.81 by tracing back available records of previous years and effect the necessary adjustments, if any;
b. draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the accumulated variance of P2.56 in the General Fund books by debiting Deferred RPT account and crediting Prior Year’s Adjustments both by the same amount; and
c. round off into two decimal places all collections of RPT before uploading onto e-NGAS to prevent the accumulation of minor variances in centavos.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to exert extra efforts in locating documents that will support the balances of Deferred RPT account of P208,821.81 recorded under the Trust Fund books to facilitate the reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts RPT Receivable and Deferred RPT. The Municipal Accountant will draw JEV to adjust the accumulated variance of P2.56 and will ensure rounding off collections in future uploads.
observations.reason: Securing relevant documents/records to support the noted Deferred RPT balance is still in process; the small accumulated variance adjustment needs to be effected.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to:
a. prioritize the inclusion of PPAs which are much needed by the Municipality and implementable during the year in the preparation of LDRRMFIP to strengthen its capacity to prevent and mitigate disaster risks; and
b. strictly implement the PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP to fully optimize the utilization of the Mitigation Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer committed to strictly implement the LDRRMFIP to upgrade significantly the Municipality’s disaster resiliency level. The Municipality explained that they first utilized unexpended balances of prior years recorded under Trust Liabilities – DRRMF and that other PPAs were not implemented due to COVID-19 restrictions.
observations.reason: Utilization rate decreased to 6.65% in CY 2020 from prior year; many PPAs not implemented due to COVID-19 restrictions and reprogramming of prior year balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Environment and Health Officer, to determine the errors in the charging of expenses related to fund transfers from DILG and DOH amounting to P45,000.00 and P143,555.50, respectively, and effect the necessary adjustments;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the utilization report for the fund transfer from DOH after the adjustment has been made; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized/unexpended fund transfers from the DA and DOH amounting to P1,235,800.00 and P56,328.44, respectively, to the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is to check the transactions first if not erroneously charged against the General Fund; error pertaining to the fund transfer from DOH amounting to P143,555.50 was determined and adjusted in the books. The Municipal Treasurer is still waiting for advice from the implementing offices on the amount to be returned. The Municipal Budget Officer is furnishing required documents.
observations.reason: Some adjustments have been made (e.g., DOH amount) but other reversions/returns to source agencies are still pending awaiting instructions from implementing offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly require the AOs to liquidate their CAs supported with complete documentary requirements within 30 days after return to the permanent official station in the case of local travel, and to refrain from granting additional CA to any official, officer or employee without liquidating first his/her prior CA.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant implemented stricter controls and ensured that granting additional CA without liquidation first is now prohibited by the Municipality. Delays in liquidation were attributed to COVID-19 restrictions affecting submission of supporting documents.
observations.reason: Delays in liquidation occurred due to inability of accountable officers to submit liquidation documents within the prescribed period because of COVID-19 restrictions.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. revert the Accounts Payable without valid contracts and no actual claims totaling P42,988.07 to the unappropriated surplus of the current General Fund, pursuant to Section 98 of PD No. 1445; and
b. ensure that all past due and valid obligations are completely covered by perfected contracts and duly supported with complete documentation before recording the books as Accounts Payable.
observations.managementAction: Claims are now being checked for supporting documentation and validity before recording. The Municipal Accounting Office will revert unutilized amounts to Unappropriated Surplus as applicable.
observations.reason: Error checking and validation of historical claims is ongoing to determine if amounts were erroneously charged; disposition pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to:
a. stop the practice of charging regular expenditures against the LDF, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit;

c. strictly implement the development projects included in the Annual Development Plan to fully optimize the utilization of the LDF and achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer committed to strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 on the PPAs allowed to be charged against the LDF and assured the Audit Team of the implementation of the Annual Development Plan to fully optimize the utilization of the Local Development Fund.
observations.reason: Many PPAs were not implemented due to restrictions brought by the COVID-19 pandemic; practice of charging regular expenditures to LDF to be stopped going forward.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer, assist the concerned Punong Barangays in identifying suitable open spaces for the establishment of MRFs in the remaining barangays, in compliance with Section 1, Rule XI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

observations.reason: Difficulty in finding suitable open spaces as determined by barangays; identification in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the GAD Focal Person, to allocate at least five per cent of the annual budget for GAD PPAs and ensure timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding Accomplishment Report to the Office of the Auditor within five working days from the receipt of the approved plan from the DILG and five working days from the end of January of the preceding year, respectively.
