observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine the details of the discrepancies between the balances per ledger, cashbook and bank and to effect the adjustments/corrections.

observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator ensure the full and timely utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund for purposes provided under the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 to ensure that the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes are fully met.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor require:
• The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately
determine the details of the discrepancies between the balances per ledger,
cashbook and bank and to effect the adjustments/corrections; and
• both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare a
quarterly reconciliation statement of Cash in Vault and Cash in Bank’s
general ledger and cashbook balances and to make the necessary
adjustments/corrections on the discrepancies noted. Furnish the Auditor
copy of the statement jointly certified both by the said agency officials within
15 days after the end of the quarter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements
(BRS) to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer to identify
unserviceable property, prepare the Inspection and Inventory Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), and dispose the said properties in
accordance with existing rules and regulations; and
b) instruct the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Office to prepare Deeds
of Donation or Transfer of Property for the two units of motor vehicle
transferred to Barangays.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher to
adjust the amount of stale checks in the books of accounts following the
procedures stated under Section 59, Chapter 3 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume
I, for LGUs.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal
Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the Audit
Team the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Government
utilize fully the 1% appropriation for the Local Council for the Protection of
Children for purposes provided under RA 9344 to ensure that the desired
elimination or reduction of juvenile delinquency in the Municipality is met.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to transfer the remaining balance of Property, Plant and Equipment
and Government Equity under Trust Fund to the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to address the difference between the accounts Real Property Tax
Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable and make the necessary
adjustments, furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) taking into account the adjustments made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to address the difference between the balances of the accounts Due
from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and make the necessary
adjustments, furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the Journal
Entry Voucher (JEV) taking into account the adjustments made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with copies of
contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) working days from its
perfection, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, to facilitate review of
compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations, completeness of
documentation and evaluation whether contractual covenants are not
disadvantageous to the government.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Audit Team with copies of POs and the
corresponding documents within five (5) working days from its issuance, as
required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, to facilitate timely review of
compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations and completeness of
documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) analyze the Accounts Payable account and revert to the
Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund those that proved to be
without actual claims, administrative or judicial or which is not
covered with perfected contracts on record; and
b) conduct periodic review of the Accounts Payable to ensure that the
account consists only of valid claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to request approval from the source agency to use the excess funds
for some other projects/programs, otherwise require reversion of the same to
the National Treasury as mandated by the above cited regulations.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require: • The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately determine the details of the discrepancies between the balances per ledger, cashbook and bank and to effect the adjustments/corrections; • the Municipal Accountant to prepare BRS in the prescribed format for all depository accounts and to submit to the Auditor for verification within ten (10) days after the end of each month; and • both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare a quarterly reconciliation statement of Cash in Vault and Cash in Bank’s general ledger and cashbook balances and to make the necessary adjustments/corrections on the discrepancies noted. Furnish the Auditor copy of the statement jointly certified both by the said agency officials within 15 days after the end of the quarter.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, staff of the Accounting Office, was not able to prepare BRS for CYs 2016 and 2017. Both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant failed to prepare a quarterly reconciliation statement of Cash in Bank’s general ledger and cashbook balances due to voluminous work.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer were not able to reconcile their records.

observations.managementAction: RPCPPE for Special Education Fund and Trust Fund were submitted. The OIC- Mun. Treasurer is in the process of gathering data of unserviceable properties and will accordingly prepare the IIRUP.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor maximize the utilization of the 70% allocation for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery in order to effectively anticipate, respond to and recover from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazard events or conditions.

We recommended that the Municipal Government utilize fully the 1% appropriation for the Local Council for the Protection of Children for purposes provided under RA 9344 to ensure that the desired elimination or reduction of juvenile delinquency in the Municipality is met.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor remind the Municipal Treasurer that the grant of 10% discounts on prompt payment of RPT and SET Receivables is applicable only once from January 1 to March  31 of each year except when payment of taxes was made on installment basis.   However, in installment, once the taxpayer failed to pay on time for the first quarter, interest will be charged to the whole amount of tax due and discounts for the next three quarters will not be applicable even if payments were made on time.

Also, interest on late payment shall  start  from  January,  thus,  late payment for the month of April should be charged with interest at 2% per month multiply by the  number  of  month  in  arrears  or  by  4  months (January, February, March and April) in this case.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and all the concerned officials to explain/justify the reason/s why expenditures amounting to P711,660.00 were charged against the Special Education Fund. Section 272 of RA No. 7160 as implemented by DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001, No. 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and No. 1 dated April 14, 1998 provides the allowable expenditures for SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with copies of contract/s within five (5) working days from its perfection, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, to facilitate review of compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations, completeness of documentation and evaluation whether contractual covenants are not disadvantageous to the government.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately to the Audit Team disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents enumerated in Annex K, otherwise the transaction shall be suspended/disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to request approval from the source agency to use the excess funds for some other projects/programs, otherwise require reversion of the same to the National Treasury as mandated by the above cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the Commission on Audit of the lacking documents to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the covered transactions.

We recommended that payment for various expenses should be made in  check directly to the suppliers/dealers.  Stop the practice of payment in  cash subject to reimbursement.

We also recommended that appropriate procurement procedure provided under RA No. 9184 be observed for procurement of goods and services.

Refrain from disbursing government funds through reimbursement and instead settle obligations by drawing checks payable to suppliers.

Submit immediately to the Audit Team the documentary requirements needed, of which he was determined to be the responsible official.
